Description: Use attributes or YAML for route configuration in Symfony controllers
Do: Use PHP 8 attributes for route definitions. Group routes with prefix and middleware
Dont: Define all routes in separate YAML files away from controllers
Code Bad: # config/routes.yaml with all routes in one file\n# Far from the controller code

Description: Let Symfony autowire services through constructor injection
Do: Use autowiring and autoconfigure. Each service gets its dependencies automatically
Dont: Manually configure every service in services.yaml
Code Bad:
# Manually defining every service:
services:
    App\Service\OrderService:
        arguments:
            $em: '@doctrine.orm.entity_manager'
            $logger: '@monolog.logger'
    # Repeated for every service...

Description: Use PHP 8 attributes for Doctrine entity mapping with proper relationships
Do: Define entities with typed properties and relationship attributes. Use migrations for schema

Description: Use custom repository classes for data access with Doctrine QueryBuilder
Do: Create repositories extending ServiceEntityRepository. Use QueryBuilder for complex queries
Dont: Put queries directly in controllers or use raw DQL everywhere

Description: Use Symfony Form component for input handling and validation
Do: Create FormType classes. Let Symfony handle data binding and CSRF
Dont: Manually parse request data and validate with if/else
Code Bad: $title = $request->request->get('title');\nif (empty($title)) { throw new BadRequestException(); }\n// Manual extraction for every field

Description: Use Symfony Validator with PHP 8 attributes for entity and DTO validation
Do: Apply validation attributes directly on entity/DTO properties
Code Bad: if (strlen($title) > 255) throw new Exception();\nif (empty($body)) throw new Exception();\n// Manual validation for each field

Description: Use Symfony Messenger for asynchronous message handling
Do: Define messages and handlers. Configure async transports for background processing
Dont: Process everything synchronously in the request
Code Bad: // Synchronous in controller:\n$mailer->send($email); // User waits for SMTP

Description: Use Symfony Security for authentication authorization and firewalls
Do: Configure security.yaml with firewalls providers and access control
Code Good:
# config/packages/security.yaml
security:
    password_hashers:
        Symfony\Component\Security\Core\User\PasswordAuthenticatedUserInterface: 'auto'
    providers:
        app_user_provider:
            entity:
                class: App\Entity\User
                property: email
    firewalls:
        api:
            pattern: ^/api
            stateless: true
            jwt: ~
    access_control:
        - { path: ^/api/login, roles: PUBLIC_ACCESS }
        - { path: ^/api, roles: ROLE_USER }

Description: Use Voters for complex authorization logic beyond simple role checks
Do: Create Voter classes for entity-level permissions. Use #[IsGranted] attribute
Dont: Check permissions with if/else in every controller method

Description: Use Symfony Events for decoupling side effects from main logic
Do: Create Event classes and EventSubscribers. Use #[AsEventListener] attribute
Dont: Call side effects directly in services

Description: Write functional tests for controllers using Symfony WebTestCase
Do: Use WebTestCase with createClient(). Test full request/response cycles
Dont: Test only business logic without HTTP layer

Description: Configure bundles properly using environment-specific config files
Do: Use config/packages/*.yaml for bundle config. Separate dev test and prod settings
Dont: Hardcode bundle settings or mix environment configs
Code Good:
# config/packages/doctrine.yaml
doctrine:
    dbal:
        url: '%env(resolve:DATABASE_URL)%'
    orm:
        auto_generate_proxy_classes: true
        naming_strategy: doctrine.orm.naming_strategy.underscore_number_aware
        auto_mapping: true
        mappings:
            App:
                type: attribute
                is_bundle: false
                dir: '%kernel.project_dir%/src/Entity'
                prefix: 'App\Entity'

# config/packages/test/doctrine.yaml
doctrine:
    dbal:
        url: '%env(resolve:DATABASE_TEST_URL)%'

Description: Use serialization groups to control which properties are exposed in API responses
Do: Define groups on entity properties. Use @Groups attribute. Different groups per context
Dont: Serialize entire entities exposing all fields
Code Bad: return $this->json($user);\n// Exposes password hash and internal fields

Description: Use Symfony HTTP Cache and Cache-Control headers for performance
Do: Set Cache-Control headers. Use ESI for partial caching. Use Varnish in production
Dont: No HTTP caching headers letting every request hit the backend

Description: Create CLI commands using Symfony Console component with proper input/output handling
Do: Use attributes for command definition. Define arguments and options. Use SymfonyStyle for output
Dont: Echo raw text or use print_r for CLI output
Code Bad: echo 'Starting import...\\n';\n// No structured output, no error handling, no dry-run
